prose: Fukui reactivity indices for benzaldehyde were computed using DFT at the B3LYP/def2-TZVP level (tier 'standard') via PySCF, in the gas phase, charge 0, multiplicity 1. Fukui indices were derived from finite-difference Mulliken partial charges using the neutral, N−1 cation (charge +1, mult 2), and N+1 anion (charge −1, mult 2) states. The geometry was taken as-is from the user-supplied .xyz file (no re-optimization at DFT). All SCF calculations converged; integrity checks all passed (trustworthy: true). No warnings were reported. The headline result (f⁺ maximum) identifies atom 3 (C, ring carbon adjacent to the aldehyde) as the most electrophilic site with f⁺ = 0.39938965, and atom 1 (O, carbonyl oxygen) as the most nucleophilic site with f⁻ = 0.23839906. Charge conservation: sum(f⁺) = 1.0000, sum(f⁻) = 1.0000. Plot written to: /home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/fukui-reactivity-validation/benzaldehyde/argo_claude-sonnet-4.6/20260706-174514_benzaldehyde_fukui_reactivity/agent_call_01_plot.png. CLI: /home/erobles/chem-skills/mcp_server/chemkit_engine/cli.py fukui --method dft --charge 0 --mult 1 --functional b3lyp --basis def2-tzvp --tier standard benzaldehyde.xyz
